preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context for SaaS trial users: acknowledges they already know the product, frames the page around why upgrading unlocks their full potential. Sets the Validator at ease by being transparent about intent.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: Follows orientation by articulating the specific capabilities and outcomes available on paid plans. For a Validator in awareness stage, this answers 'what exactly do I get?' before any proof is presented — giving them a concrete framework to evaluate.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators require third-party trust signals before they internalize claims. Placing credibility after value lets logos, testimonials, and authority markers validate the promises just made, building the proof scaffolding this archetype demands.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Deepens the proof layer with hard metrics, case study data, and quantified outcomes. For a Validator, this is the decisive beat — abstract value claims are now anchored in verifiable results. Positioned after credibility so social proof primes receptivity to data.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Awareness-stage conversion is kept soft: a low-friction CTA such as 'Explore your upgrade options' or 'See what's in your paid plan.' No Decision beat per awareness rules — avoids premature pricing pressure on trial users who are still validating fit.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Provides secondary paths for Validators who need more time: links to documentation, comparison guides, or a 'talk to sales' option. Ensures no trial user hits a dead end, preserving re-engagement opportunities for those not yet ready to commit.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes immediate context for SaaS trial users: acknowledges they already know the product, frames the page around why upgrading unlocks their full potential. Sets the Validator at ease by being transparent about intent.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validators require third-party trust signals before they internalize claims. Placing credibility after value lets logos, testimonials, and authority markers validate the promises just made, building the proof scaffolding this archetype demands.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. Awareness stage requires focused conversion intent.
Validator variant always includes trust_signal. with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster, directly supporting validator archetype needs.

slots.headline: Your design system renders. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: The semantic intelligence layer that resolves component behavior, visual emphasis, and content register — automatically, for every user and every moment.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator variant leads with quote, logos below. This establishes credibility through both narrative and recognizable brand endorsement for trial user conversion.

slots.headline: Used by teams who ship in code, not Figma comments.
slots.quote: We eliminated an entire class of variant QA. The resolution engine makes the right call by default — we stopped arguing about which component state to use and started shipping.
slots.attribution: Senior product engineer, B2B SaaS team — 2024 customer survey

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows. Critical for converting trial users in awareness stage.
